revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Sada Jinja
佐太神社 | image = Sada jinja.jpg | width = | caption = Sada Jinja | type = | dedication = | founded = | closed = | founder = | priest = | address = | phone = | website = http://sadajinjya.jp/index.php }} {{nihongo|'''Sada Jinja'''|佐太神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Matsue, Shimane|Matsue]], [[Shimane Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The [[Taisha-zukuri]] north, central and south halls of 1807 are [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan|Important Cultural Properties]].{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=102&item_id=2966 |title=Sada Jinja Hakuden |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}}{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=102&item_id=2965 |title=Sada Jinja Seichūden |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}}{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=102&item_id=2967 |title=Sada Jinja Nanden |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}} Sada Shin [[Noh]], ritual purification dances performed annually on 24 and 25 September, have been designated an [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=302&item_id=114 |title=Sada Shin Noh |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}} In 2011 Sada Shin Noh was inscribed on the [[UNESCO]] [[UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity]].{{cite web |url=http://www.unesco.org/culture/ich/index.php?lg=en&pg=00011&RL=00412 |title=Sada Shin Noh, sacred dancing at Sada shrine, Shimane |publisher=[[UNESCO]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}} ==See also== {{Commons category|Kamosu-jinja}} * [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * [[Yaegaki Shrine|Yaegaki Jinja]] * [[Kamosu Jinja]] * [[Izumo Taisha]] * [[UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== * {{ja icon}} [http://sadajinjya.jp/index.php Sada Jinja homepage] {{Shinto shrine}} [[ja:佐太神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Masterpieces of the Oral and Intangible Heritage of Humanity]] [[Category:Noh]] {{Japan-struct-stub}}

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Sada Jinja
佐太神社 | image = Sada jinja.jpg | caption = Sada Jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|30|32|N|133|0|23|E|display=title,inline|type:landmark_region:JP_scale:10000}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = {{URL|http://sadajinjya.jp/index.php}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Sada Jinja'''|佐太神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Matsue, Shimane|Matsue]], [[Shimane Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The [[Taisha-zukuri]] north, central and south halls of 1807 are [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan|Important Cultural Properties]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2966 |title=Sada Jinja Hakuden |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2965 |title=Sada Jinja Seichūden |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}}{{cite web dances performed annually on 24 and 25 September, have been designated an [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/114 |title=Sada Shin Noh |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}} In 2011 Sada Shin [[Noh]] was inscribed on the [[UNESCO]] [[UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity]].{{cite web 佐太神社 | image = Sada jinja.jpg | caption = Sada Jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|30|32|N|133|0|23|E|display=title,inline|type:landmark_region:JP_scale:10000}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = {{URL|http://sadajinjya.jp/index.php}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Sada Jinja'''|佐太神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Matsue, Shimane|Matsue]], [[Shimane Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The [[Taisha-zukuri]] north, central and south halls of 1807 are [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan|Important Cultural Properties]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2966 |title=Sada Jinja Hakuden |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2965 |title=Sada Jinja Seichūden |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2967 |title=Sada Jinja Nanden |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}} '''Sada Shin Noh''', ritual purification dances performed annually on 24 and 25 September, have been designated an [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/114 |title=Sada Shin Noh |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}} In 2011 Sada Shin [[Noh]] was inscribed on the [[UNESCO]] [[UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity]].{{cite web |url=http://www.unesco.org/culture/ich/index.php?lg=en&pg=00011&RL=00412 |title=Sada Shin Noh, sacred dancing at Sada shrine, Shimane |publisher=[[UNESCO]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}} ==See also== * [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * [[Yaegaki Shrine|Yaegaki Jinja]] * [[Kamosu Shrine]] * [[Izumo Taisha]] * [[UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== * {{in lang|ja}} [http://sadajinjya.jp/index.php Sada Jinja The [[Taisha-zukuri]] north, central and south halls of 1807 are [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan|Important Cultural Properties]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2966 |title=Sada Jinja Hakuden |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2965 |title=Sada Jinja Seichūden |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2967 |title=Sada Jinja Nanden |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}} '''Sada Shin Noh''', ritual purification dances performed annually on 24 and 25 September, have been designated an [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/114 |title=Sada Shin Noh |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}} In 2011 Sada Shin [[Noh]] was inscribed on the [[UNESCO]] [[UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity]].{{cite web |url=http://www.unesco.org/culture/ich/index.php?lg=en&pg=00011&RL=00412 |title=Sada Shin Noh, sacred dancing at Sada shrine, Shimane |publisher=[[UNESCO]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}} ==See also== * The [[Taisha-zukuri]] north, central and south halls of 1807 are [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan|Important Cultural Properties]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2966 |title=Sada Jinja Hakuden |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}}{{cite web dances performed annually on 24 and 25 September, have been designated an [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/114 |title=Sada Shin Noh |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}} In 2011 Sada Shin [[Noh]] was inscribed on the [[UNESCO]] [[UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity]].{{cite web {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Sada Jinja
佐太神社 | image = Sada jinja.jpg | caption = Sada Jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|30|32|N|133|0|23|E|display=title,inline|type:landmark_region:JP_scale:10000}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = {{URL|http://sadajinjya.jp/index.php}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Sada Jinja'''|佐太神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Matsue, Shimane|Matsue]], [[Shimane Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The [[Taisha-zukuri]] north, central and south halls of 1807 are [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan|Important Cultural Properties]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2966 |title=Sada Jinja Hakuden |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2965 |title=Sada Jinja Seichūden |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2967 |title=Sada Jinja Nanden |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}} '''Sada Shin Noh''', ritual purification dances performed annually on 24 and 25 September, have been designated an [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/114 |title=Sada Shin Noh |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}} In 2011 Sada Shin [[Noh]] was inscribed on the [[UNESCO]] [[UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity]].{{cite web |url=http://www.unesco.org/culture/ich/index.php?lg=en&pg=00011&RL=00412 |title=Sada Shin Noh, sacred dancing at Sada shrine, Shimane |publisher=[[UNESCO]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}} ==See also== * {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Sada Jinja
佐太神社 | image = Sada jinja.jpg | caption = Sada Jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|30|32|N|133|0|23|E|display=title,inline|type:landmark_region:JP_scale:10000}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = {{URL|http://sadajinjya.jp/index.php}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Sada Jinja'''|佐太神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Matsue, Shimane|Matsue]], [[Shimane Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The [[Taisha-zukuri]] north, central and south halls of 1807 are [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan|Important Cultural Properties]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2966 |title=Sada Jinja Hakuden |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2965 |title=Sada Jinja Seichūden |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2967 |title=Sada Jinja Nanden |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}} '''Sada Shin Noh''', ritual purification dances performed annually on 24 and 25 September, have been designated an [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/114 |title=Sada Shin Noh |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}} In 2011 Sada Shin [[Noh]] was inscribed on the [[UNESCO]] [[UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity]].{{cite web |url=http://www.unesco.org/culture/ich/index.php?lg=en&pg=00011&RL=00412 |title=Sada Shin Noh, sacred dancing at Sada shrine, Shimane |publisher=[[UNESCO]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}} ==See also== * [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * [[Yaegaki Shrine|Yaegaki Jinja]] * [[Kamosu Shrine]] * [[Izumo Taisha]] * [[UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== * {{in lang|ja}} [http://sadajinjya.jp/index.php Sada Jinja 佐太神社 | image = Sada jinja.jpg | caption = Sada Jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|30|32|N|133|0|23|E|display=title,inline|type:landmark_region:JP_scale:10000}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = {{URL|http://sadajinjya.jp/index.php}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Sada Jinja'''|佐太神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]], [[Shimane Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The [[Taisha-zukuri]] north, central and south halls of 1807 are [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2966 |title=Sada Jinja Hakuden |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2965 |title=Sada Jinja Seichūden |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2967 |title=Sada Jinja Nanden |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}} '''Sada Shin Noh''', ritual purification dances performed annually on 24 and 25 September, have been designated an [[:en:List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/114 |title=Sada Shin Noh |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}} In 2011 Sada Shin [[:en:Noh|Noh]] was inscribed on the [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity]].{{cite web The [[Taisha-zukuri]] north, central and south halls of 1807 are [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]].{{cite web [[co:commons:Category:Sada-jinja]][[en:Sada Shrine]][[fa:سادا جینجا]][[fr:Sada-jinja]][[ja:佐太神社]][[mk:Храм Сада]][[uk:Храм Сада]] [[co:commons:Category:Sada-jinja]][[en:Sada Shrine]][[fa:سادا جینجا]][[fr:Sada-jinja]][[ja:佐太神社]][[mk:Храм Сада]][[uk:Храм Сада]] [[en:Sada Shrine]][[fa:سادا جینجا]][[fr:Sada-jinja]][[ja:佐太神社]][[mk:Храм Сада]][[uk:Храм Сада]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Sada Jinja
佐太神社 | image = File:Sada jinja.jpg | caption = Sada Jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.508917|N|133.006444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = {{URL|http://sadajinjya.jp/index.php}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Matsue|WD=Q207321|1=Matsue}} | part_of = {{ill|Izumo Shinbutsu Pilgrimage|WD=Q11395860|1=Izumo Shinbutsu Pilgrimage}} }} {{nihongo|'''Sada Jinja'''|佐太神社}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|WD=Q207321|lt=Matsue|1=Matsue}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The [[Taisha-zukuri]] north, central and south halls of 1807 are {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2966 |title=Sada Jinja Hakuden |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2965 |title=Sada Jinja Seichūden |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2967 |title=Sada Jinja Nanden |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}} '''Sada Shin Noh''', ritual purification dances performed annually on 24 and 25 September, have been designated an {{ill|List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|en|List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|WD=Q111360896|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties}}.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/114 |title=Sada Shin Noh |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=31 January 2012}} In 2011 Sada Shin {{ill|Noh|en|Noh|WD=Q226887|lt=Noh|1=Noh}} was inscribed on the {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|en|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|WD=Q4435332|lt=Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity|1=UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists}}.{{cite web |url=http://www.unesco.org/culture/ich/index.php?lg=en&pg=00011&RL=00412 |title=Sada Shin Noh, sacred dancing at Sada shrine, Shimane |publisher={{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} |accessdate=31 January 2012}} ==See also== * {{ill|List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|en|List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|WD=Q111360896|lt=List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|1=List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties}} * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] * [[Yaegaki Shrine|Yaegaki Jinja]] * {{ill|Kamosu Shrine|en|Kamosu Shrine|WD=Q3192477|lt=Kamosu Shrine|1=Kamosu Shrine}} * [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] * {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|en|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|WD=Q4435332|lt=Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity|1=UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage
